title: Compare two sources
subtitle: Same topic, different focus.
overview: Module: Source Reaction. B2 Bridge moves from strong B1 output into abstract thinking, source reaction, nuanced opinions, natural style, and longer spoken independence.

sections.title: B2 bridge focus
rows: Comparing sources helps you see how different writers frame the same issue.
比较来源能帮助你看到不同作者如何构建同一问题。
B2 bridge frame: summarize the source, evaluate it, connect it to experience, and respond.
B2 Bridge 框架：总结来源、评价它、连接经验，并做出回应。
Compare two short sources on one topic.
比较同一话题的两个短来源。
Both sources discuss speaking practice, but the first focuses on confidence, while the second focuses on pronunciation and rhythm. Together, they show that speaking is both emotional and technical.
两个来源都讨论口语练习，但第一个关注自信，而第二个关注发音和节奏。放在一起，它们说明口语既是情绪性的，也是技术性的。

items.en: Comparing sources helps you see how different writers frame the same issue.
items.zh: 比较来源能帮助你看到不同作者如何构建同一问题。

items.en: B2 bridge frame: summarize the source, evaluate it, connect it to experience, and respond.
items.zh: B2 Bridge 框架：总结来源、评价它、连接经验，并做出回应。

items.en: Both sources discuss speaking practice, but the first focuses on confidence, while the second focuses on pronunciation and rhythm. Together, they show that speaking is both emotional and technical.
items.zh: 两个来源都讨论口语练习，但第一个关注自信，而第二个关注发音和节奏。放在一起，它们说明口语既是情绪性的，也是技术性的。

items.en: I will not rush to sound advanced; I will make the idea clear first.
items.zh: 我不会急着显得高级；我会先把意思说清楚。

items.en: Then I will add nuance: a condition, a limit, a contrast, or a counterpoint.
items.zh: 然后我会加入细腻度：条件、限制、对比或反方观点。

items.en: If the sentence feels too heavy, I can paraphrase it in simpler spoken English.
items.zh: 如果句子感觉太沉重，我可以用更简单的口语英语改说。

items.en: A B2 answer should sound organized, flexible, and alive.
items.zh: B2 回答应该听起来有组织、灵活、有生命力。

items.en: I will record one answer and improve the structure, not only the grammar.
items.zh: 我会录一个回答，并改进结构，而不只是语法。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Compare two short sources on one topic.
lines.zh: 比较同一话题的两个短来源。

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Both sources discuss speaking practice, but the first focuses on confidence, while the second focuses on pronunciation and rhythm. Together, they show that speaking is both emotional and technical.
lines.zh: 两个来源都讨论口语练习，但第一个关注自信，而第二个关注发音和节奏。放在一起，它们说明口语既是情绪性的，也是技术性的。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Can you add nuance or a limitation?
lines.zh: 你能加入细腻度或限制条件吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Yes. I will add a condition, a contrast, or one fair counterpoint.
lines.zh: 可以。我会加入一个条件、一个对比或一个公平的反方观点。

items.en: Compare two short sources on one topic.
items.zh: 比较同一话题的两个短来源。

items.en: Answer once in four sentences, then expand it to six or seven sentences.
items.zh: 先用四句话回答一次，然后扩展到六七句话。

items.en: Add one phrase that shows nuance: generally, however, that said, or it depends.
items.zh: 加入一个显示细腻度的短语：generally、however、that said 或 it depends。

items.en: Record the answer and check whether the structure is easy to follow.
items.zh: 录下回答，并检查结构是否容易跟上。

items.en: Reuse the best sentence tomorrow in a new topic.
items.zh: 明天在一个新话题中再次使用最好的句子。
